title: ACWA Power Signs Deals to Boost Hydrogen and Ammonia Exports to EU
content: ACWA Power, under the supervision of Saudi Arabiaâ€™s Ministry of Energy and in the presence of Minister Prince Abdulaziz bin Salman, signed a series of agreements and MoUs with major European energy firms to establish a green hydrogen and renewable energy export value chain from Saudi Arabia to Europe. Key partners include Edison S.p.A., TotalEnergies Renewables, Zhero Europe, and EnBW. The agreements aim to evaluate demand and feasibility for large-scale renewables and develop transmission corridors, supported by technical partners such as Prysmian, Siemens Energy, GE Vernova, Hitachi, and CESI. A Joint Development Agreement with EnBW will initiate the Yanbu Green Hydrogen Hub, expected to be operational by 2030. This integrated facility will include renewable power, electrolysis, ammonia conversion, and export terminals. The initiative supports Saudi Arabiaâ€™s leadership in the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC) and aligns with Vision 2030 goals for energy diversification and global clean energy trade.
author: Analysts Account
